safe_checks: After a qualifying push-domain clock edge and the specified one-cycle local reset-registration delay, asserting either push_rst=1 or push_sender_in_reset=1 must place the write side in reset and push_receiver_in_reset must indicate that reset state.

After a qualifying pop-domain clock edge and the specified one-cycle local reset-registration delay, asserting pop_rst=1 must place the read side in reset; no normal dequeue obligation should be enforced while the read side is in reset.
While the write side is in reset, pushes must not be treated as accepted writes.
A pop completes only on a pop_clk edge where pop_valid=1 and pop_ready=1.
No item may be popped unless it was previously accepted on the push side.
Accepted items must be popped in FIFO order with no duplication and no skipping.
When pop_valid=1 and pop_ready=0, pop_data must remain stable and the identity of the next item to be popped must remain unchanged until a later successful pop handshake or reset.
push_credit must never pulse unless there was a prior successful pop handshake.
Each successful pop handshake may produce at most one returned push_credit pulse in the push clock domain.
When push_credit_stall=1, immediate credit pulse generation is blocked; therefore the testbench must not require a credit pulse during stalled intervals.

push_full and pop_empty are legal status indicators for no-space / no-data conditions, but their exact transition cycles must be treated cautiously because remote-pointer visibility is synchronized across domains.
Occupancy-related exposed values must remain within the representable range 0 through 17 when they are checked in clearly stable states.
unsafe_checks: Do not strictly assert an exact cycle latency from a push-side write to pop_valid/pop_data visibility.
Do not strictly assert same-cycle cut-through from push_valid/push_data to pop_valid/pop_data when the FIFO is empty.
Do not strictly assert an exact cycle latency from a successful pop to push_credit pulse assertion.
Do not strictly assert exact cycle-by-cycle values of push_full or pop_empty around CDC boundary crossings.
Do not strictly assert exact cycle-by-cycle arithmetic values for push_slots, credit_count_push, credit_available_push, or pop_items across domains.
Do not strictly assert any particular saturation, wraparound, or signedness behavior for credit_available_push when credit_withhold_push exceeds credit_count_push.
Do not strictly assert acceptance or rejection behavior for illegal push_valid attempts when the FIFO is full unless acceptance is externally observable through later ordered pops.
Do not strictly assert combinational reset effects before the qualifying local clock edge plus the stated one-cycle delayed internal effect.
Do not strictly assert pop_valid=0 or a specific pop_data value in empty-edge cut-through situations where no successful pop occurs and the exact preview timing is unspecified.
Do not strictly assert exact pointer-synchronizer timing or any internal CDC implementation detail.
minimal_safe_oracle: Reset oracle: verify one-local-clock delayed synchronous reset behavior on each domain, including that push_sender_in_reset participates in the push-side reset condition and that pushes during push-side reset are ignored.
Queue oracle: maintain a reference queue of definitely accepted writes and require every successful pop handshake to return the oldest queued byte, with no duplicates, skips, or spontaneous pops.
Backpressure oracle: once pop_valid=1 and pop_ready=0, require pop_data to stay unchanged and forbid dequeue progress until a later successful pop handshake or reset.

Credit oracle: count successful pops and observed push_credit pulses; strictly require no credit without a prior pop and no more than one credit pulse per pop, then require one-for-one eventual correspondence only over long unstalled windows without imposing fixed latency.

reset_tests.description: Push-domain synchronous reset delay with idle interfaces. Hold push_valid=0 and pop_ready=0. Drive push_rst=1, tick push_clk once, then check that the reset effect is not required until after the stated internal one-cycle delay; after the delayed effect, require push_receiver_in_reset=1 and require that any pushes attempted while reset remains active are not later observable as popped data.

push_sender_in_reset must behave like push-side reset. Keep push_rst=0, assert push_sender_in_reset=1, hold push_valid=0 and pop_ready=0, advance push_clk, then after the delayed effect require push_receiver_in_reset=1. While push_sender_in_reset stays high, attempt one or more pushes and later verify they never emerge on the pop side.

Push-side reset release. Deassert both push_rst and push_sender_in_reset, wait through the local reset-registration delay, then perform a small write sequence and verify those post-release writes can later be observed in FIFO order on the pop side.

Pop-domain synchronous reset delay. Prefill the FIFO with known data first, then assert pop_rst=1 while pop_ready=0, tick pop_clk, and only after the delayed internal reset effect stop applying strict normal-dequeue expectations. After deasserting pop_rst and waiting through the delay, verify subsequent pop handshakes again follow FIFO ordering for data written after reset release.

Independent-domain reset interaction. Reset only the push side while leaving the pop side active, and separately reset only the pop side while leaving the push side active. Strictly check only local reset obligations, queue legality, and absence of impossible pops; avoid exact status/latency checks during cross-domain recovery.

normal_tests.description: Single write then single read with independent clocks. Write one byte A5 on push side after reset release, wait until pop_valid becomes true, then perform one pop handshake and require pop_data=A5 on the handshake cycle. Track that exactly one queue element was removed.

Multiple writes then multiple reads in FIFO order. Push a short ordered sequence such as 11, 22, 33, 44 under push_clk, then later pop them one by one under pop_clk and require the same order at the successful pop handshakes.
Interleaved push/pop traffic with unrelated clock ratios. Alternate bursts of pushes and pops while maintaining a reference queue. This separates CDC ordering bugs from simple single-burst behavior.

vectors.expected: all successful pops match the oldest accepted outstanding write

normal_tests.description: Backpressure hold. Make pop_valid become high for a known head item, then hold pop_ready=0 across several pop_clk cycles. Strictly require pop_data to remain constant and require no dequeue progress until pop_ready is finally asserted.

vectors.expected: 0x5A remains presented throughout stall; first successful pop returns 0x5A, second later returns 0xC3

normal_tests.description: Credit generation without fixed latency. Perform K successful pops with push_credit_stall=0 for a long unstalled interval afterward. Strictly require no push_credit before any pop, no more than K total pulses attributable to those pops, and eventual total observed pulses equals K by the end of the observation window.

vectors.expected: exactly 3 total push_credit pulses observed by end of long unstalled window, with no fixed-cycle placement required

corner_case_tests.description: Data boundary patterns. Use all-zeros, all-ones, alternating, and walking-edge values to catch wrong bit slicing or stale-data bugs.

Depth-boundary stress near 0, 16, and 17 entries. Fill with 16 items, then 17 items, then drain back to 1 and 0 using long observation windows. Strictly check FIFO order and absence of duplication/loss; use push_full/pop_empty only as secondary stable-state checks after waiting for CDC settling.

vectors.expected: ordered delivery for all accepted items; optional stable-state check after settling: full at 17, empty at 0

corner_case_tests.description: Attempted overfill scenario. After reaching an apparently full stable condition and waiting extra synchronization time, continue pulsing push_valid with distinct data. Do not strictly assume whether illegal pushes are accepted immediately at the interface; instead discriminate by checking that later successful pops never exceed FIFO capacity worth of accepted items and preserve order of definitely accepted data.

Empty-side behavior under pop_ready activity. With no previously accepted data and after a long empty-settling period, toggle pop_ready for multiple pop_clk edges. Strictly require that no pop handshake is counted unless pop_valid is actually high, and require no credit pulses attributable to readiness alone.

vectors.expected: no queue removal and no credit without a true pop_valid&&pop_ready handshake

corner_case_tests.description: Credit stall behavior. Perform several successful pops while push_credit_stall=1 during the likely return interval, then release stall. Strictly require no push_credit pulse while stall is asserted and no more than one pulse per pop overall. Treat exact deferred replay timing and whether deferred credits are replayed versus suppressed as ambiguous unless the implementation makes it externally clear over a long window.

Reset overlap with queued data. Queue a few items, then assert push-side reset or pop-side reset mid-stream. Strictly require reset-local behavior, discard any pre-reset exact latency expectations, and after reset release verify clean post-reset operation with newly written data only.

sequence_tests.description: Full ordered-traffic traversal across both domains. Release resets, push 6 distinct bytes, pop 2, push 4 more, stall consumer for several cycles while head item is visible, then pop the remainder. Maintain an explicit reference queue and require every successful pop to match queue head. This sequence exposes stale outputs, wrong dequeue edge, ordering bugs, and backpressure violations.

Clock-ratio sweep sequence. Repeat the same push/pop script under three regimes: push_clk faster than pop_clk, pop_clk faster than push_clk, and relatively prime clocks. Use the same queue oracle and credit counter oracle. This targets CDC bugs that only appear for specific phase relationships.

Near-full then drain sequence. Push until 17 accepted items are believed resident after long settling, optionally observe stable full indication, then pop all 17 while checking exact data order on each handshake and eventual return to an empty stable condition after settling. This targets off-by-one depth bugs at 16/17 and missing last-item behavior.

Hold-then-release head item sequence. Arrange for one item to be visible at the pop side, keep pop_ready=0 for multiple pop_clk edges, then pulse unrelated pushes in the other domain, and finally release pop_ready. Strictly require the held head item to remain the next item popped despite unrelated push activity.

Credit accounting sequence. Pop N known items with push_credit_stall toggled through phases 0,1,0. Count successful pops and credit pulses over a long run. Strictly require no credit without prior pop, no more than one credit per pop, and no pulses during the stalled phase. Use the final pulse count over a generous unstalled tail as the discriminator rather than any specific return latency.

Reset-state transition sequence. Exercise push_rst assertion/deassertion, push_sender_in_reset assertion/deassertion, and pop_rst assertion/deassertion at different times while otherwise idle first, then with queued traffic. The expected-state trace is local-domain only: each reset takes effect after a local clock plus one-cycle internal delay, overrides normal local behavior while active, and post-release operation resumes only after the delay.

random_tests.description: Randomized dual-clock protocol test with constrained stimulus. Randomize push_valid, pop_ready, push_data, push_credit_stall, occasional push_sender_in_reset pulses, occasional push_rst/pop_rst pulses, and independent push/pop clock periods or phase offsets. Maintain only the safe oracle: local delayed-reset blocking, reference queue ordering on successful pops, pop_data stability under backpressure, and credit pulse count implications without fixed latency. Bias random generation toward long backpressure streaks, bursts near full/empty boundaries, and repeated data values to catch stale-output and duplicate-pop mutants.

key_discriminators: push_sender_in_reset must keep the write side in reset just like push_rst; mutants that ignore it will leak writes that later pop out.
Reset effects are synchronous and locally delayed by one cycle; mutants that reset immediately, asynchronously, or with wrong polarity will fail the reset sequences.
A dequeue occurs only on pop_valid&&pop_ready at pop_clk; mutants that advance on pop_ready alone or on pop_valid alone will fail empty-side and backpressure tests.
Under pop_valid&& !pop_ready, pop_data must stay stable and the head item must not change; this catches stale-state advancement and data-loss bugs.
FIFO ordering across interleaved dual-clock traffic is the primary discriminator for pointer, wraparound, and wrong-edge bugs.
Near-full and full-depth sequences at 16 and 17 entries target off-by-one full/occupancy logic without overspecifying exact status timing.
Credit checking should be count-based and latency-insensitive; this catches extra-credit, missing-credit, and credit-on-ready bugs without assuming exact CDC timing.
Using long unstalled tails after pops separates legitimate delayed credit return from implementations that never return credits.
Holding a visible head item while new pushes occur distinguishes proper pop-side retention from designs that incorrectly preview or overwrite the head under backpressure.
Stable-state optional checks after generous settling can catch obvious broken push_full/pop_empty implementations while avoiding ambiguous CDC-edge timing.
